note: TASK 54B §1 — THE BINARY VERDICT. Self-reported confidence scalars are the judge's mannerism, not a measurement. G16a/b/c verdicts are the conjunction of three forced binaries; the scalar is requested and logged to the yellow ledger as a diagnostic and DECIDES NOTHING. These texts are pinned byte-stable: tooth 11 (the calibration probe) hashes this file against its pinned constant and re-proves perfect separation on the sealed probe set whenever a text changes. Amending any text bumps `protocol` (§4 — the cache freshness law), which lawfully re-judges affected plates once. {PROSE} is the only substitution. p3 (§2 calibration): p2's action_matches and no_contradiction read a still tableau like a filmed deed — four true pairs fell to literalism (the p2 table is in LOOP_LOG). p3 judges BELONGING: a different DEED fails, a different instant does not; a visible OPPOSITE fails, an omission does not. p4: one true caption fell at p3 to a LOOKALIKE denial — a wayside blaze in frame was read as denying a told banked hearth. The opposite must concern the VERY THING the words name; a lookalike elsewhere in the scene denies nothing.

questions.moment:
This image was painted to illustrate the story moment quoted below.
MOMENT: "{PROSE}"
Judge it with three forced binaries — each strictly true or false, no middle ground:
1) element_present — name ONE concrete visual element that the MOMENT's own words state and that you can SEE in this image (a stated place, figure, creature, object, weather, light, or act made visible). True only if you can name such an element from the text; if nothing the text states is visible, answer false and put the closest missing thing in element.
2) action_matches — the image does not stage a DIFFERENT deed than the MOMENT tells. A painted moment is one still instant: the scene may sit just before, during, or after the told deed, and quiet framing of it (the road walked, the door faced, the aftermath) is the SAME deed. If the MOMENT states no action, answer true. Answer false ONLY if the image clearly stages some OTHER deed than the MOMENT's — a different act entirely, not a different instant or framing of the told one.
3) no_contradiction — nothing visible DENIES the MOMENT's words. Answer false only when the image shows the OPPOSITE of something stated: a plainly different place than the one named, bright day against told night, a season against the stated one, a figure whose face or head the words dwell on cut out of frame, ruin where the words say it stands whole, or the told cast replaced by different people. The opposite must be shown of the VERY THING the words name: a fire burning elsewhere in frame does not deny a told banked hearth, a second door or road elsewhere denies nothing, and a lookalike of a named thing in some other part of the scene is not that thing shown opposite. A stated detail merely NOT SHOWN — unseen weather, an absent prop, fewer figures than named, a deed not caught in this instant — is an omission, and an omission NEVER contradicts.
Also report element (the element you named) and confidence 0..1. Confidence is a diagnostic only — it decides nothing.
